summary: Script renaming to unsaved state until project reload

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.beta2.official [a013481b0]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta2 - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 9 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-11900K @ 3.50GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

I thought I had figured out how to make an MRP for this: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/102319

However, in so doing I noticed another bug, but not sure if it is related to the other issue. Where I had the script named one thing, renamed it, then renamed it again, and it went into this unsaved state, even though the file was saved.

After that script bug, the rest of the video was me trying to recreate the other issues crash. I stop the video after a time, to save on file size for the upload, but I just test without success trying to recreate the crash.

### Steps to reproduce

At about 2 minutes in you can see the script in the editor panel go from the filename to unsaved

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e42ba340-73d8-49bc-ba55-4c7092c99e79

The first output for the script glitch was this. I had opened my other project, quit to list and then created a new project, this is just the output from the new project:

summary: TreeItem button tooltip text trigger area is shifted to the right

description.text:
### Tested versions

- v4.2.2.stable.official [15073afe3] **works**
- v4.3.dev1.official [9d1cbab1c] **works**
- v4.3.dev2.official [352434668] **works**
- v4.3.dev3.official [36e943b6b] **works**
- v4.3.dev4.official [df78c0636] **works**
- v4.3.dev5.official [89f70e98d] **works**
- v4.3.dev6.official [89850d553] **fails**
- v4.3.beta1.official [a4f2ea91a] **fails**
- v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8] **fails**
- v4.4.beta2.official [a013481b0] **fails**

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta2 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1080 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6614) - AMD Ryzen 9 7900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads)

### Issue description

When adding buttons to a tree with tooltips, the tooltip should show up. Under specific circumstanced this will not happen. This is a regression since `v4.3.dev6`.

I have multiple columns in a tree, with many columns and one last column with buttons. The button column is set to not expanding. The button have all tooltips set. The tooltips trigger area are all shifted to the right, the most left button will show up on the next button or only after half of the button. Smaller buttons will increase this problem.

If you take a look at the next image, i marked the area in yellow where the tooltip will not show up.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f2bdf782-7231-4659-a5cf-6a6205a32343)

### Steps to reproduce

Just hover over the buttons, from left to right with the mouse. On the first and third tree the tooltip will show up later (more to the right).

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[tempgodot.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18660600/tempgodot.zip)

description.text:
### Tested versions - Reproducible in latest master (eee39f004) - Not reproducible in 4.4 beta 2 ### System information Godot v4.4.beta (eee39f004) - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 580 2048SP (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 31.0.21921.1000) - AMD Ryzen 5 3600 6-Core Processor (12 threads) ### Issue description When you have two or more scenes open with unsaved changes and attempt to close the editor, selecting `Don't save` lock the engine from be closed or restarted. After select `Don't save` these three errors will appear: ``` ERROR: Can't add child '@ConfirmationDialog@8238' to itself. at: (C:\Users\Matheus\Downloads\Godot Source\scene/main/node.cpp:1652) ERROR: Condition "!windows.has(p_window)" is true. Returning: -1 at: DisplayServerWindows::window_get_current_screen (platform\windows\display_server_windows.cpp:1959) ERROR: Condition "!is_inside_tree()" is true. at: Viewport::_push_unhandled_input_internal (C:\Users\Matheus\Downloads\Godot Source\scene/main/viewport.cpp:3338) ```
And any new attempt to close/restart the editor or close a scene will throw this error: ``` ERROR: Condition "!is_inside_tree()" is true. at: Window::popup_centered (C:\Users\Matheus\Downloads\Godot Source\scene/main/window.cpp:1794) ``` https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7c028dd3-1eef-4439-94e5-a8e0f11d695c
Bisecting points to #102070 as the culprit ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/216e3ef0-b86e-4650-b1a2-4563b1fe2c48) CC @ryevdokimov ### Steps to reproduce 1. Open two scenes at the same time 2. Do any modification in both scenes but don't save 3. Try to close/restart the editor 4. In the confirmation window, choice `Don't save` ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: Light Attenuation gets cut off near the range boundry in 4.4b2

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.4beta2, 4.4dev7
- Not reproduce-able in 4.3.stable, 4.2.1.stable

### System information

Windows 11 - Godot v4.4.beta2 - Vulcan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 3060

### Issue description

Lights in Godot4.4beta2 seem to have their brightness sharply cut off near the edge of the light, this doesn't happen in Godot4.3
This affects both omni and spotlights

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f1eeb42e-4b7b-401e-82e3-d58f227b4977)
left Godot4.3, right Godot4.4b2 



### Steps to reproduce

Place an omnilight node in the scene and adjust its attenuation. 
Note the edge of the light, attenuation should change the rate of fade-out from the edge, a true hard cutoff should only be visible at values deep in the negative. 

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[LightAttenuationTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18659065/LightAttenuationTest.zip)

summary: Text inside `Label` nodes doesn't account `&zwsp` (`U+200B`) as line-breaking space

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.3-stable-web

### System information

Linux 6.11.11-1-MANJARO

### Issue description

Text inside `Label` nodes doesn't account `&zwsp` (`U+200B`) as line-breaking space. Making custom line-breaking algorithms impossible to implement.

### Steps to reproduce

- Put any text that has `&zwsp` (`U+200B`) inside for manual line-breaking.
- Put `autowrap_mode` to `Word`.
- Non-latin strings have abnormal proportion despite having `&zwsp` applied.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

No reproduction project, but there's sample text that includes `&zwsp`:

The Create New Node dialog does not include custom controls if the control script is within the editor plugin folder and the plugin is disabled. ### Issue description

When using _validate_property to hide `@export_tool_button`, the button is not hidden in the inspector.

```gdscript
func _validate_property(property: Dictionary):

```

I tried using other types like PROPERTY_USAGE_READ_ONLY and they work.
I was able to work around the issue by using PROPERTY_USAGE_HIGH_END_GFX to hide it but I don't think this would work in all cases, depending on the project.

```gdscript
@tool
extends Node2D

@export_tool_button("Action") var _action_button = action

func action():
	print("action pressed")

func _validate_property(property: Dictionary):
	if property.name == "_action_button":
		property.usage |= PROPERTY_USAGE_NONE
```

_action_button should not be displayed in the inspector but it is.

summary: VoxelGI baking incorrectly when objects are rotated

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.1, 4.3, 4.4 beta1, 4.4 beta2 
- These are the only versions I tried

### System information

Windows 11 - RTX 4080 - Vulkan, MacOS - M4 - Vulkan

### Issue description

When baking a VoxelGI node, its results are completely off when an object within its bounds has any form of rotation. For example, consider a wall piece that is symetrical on both sides. The result of the voxel GI bake will be completely different if the wall has a 0 degree y-rotation or a 180 degree y-rotation. Objects that have a 90 degree or -90 degree rotation are even more problematic, since they cannot be rotated in any way to get a correct result:

Screenshots:
For all screenshots I set the Display mode in editor to VoxelGI Lighting.

Screenshot 1 - incorrect bake:  
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e56fe111-b410-4d14-a22f-a492fc9c32c7)
Notice the left wall is rotated 180 degrees along the y-axis.

Screenshot 2 - correct bake:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3911ea82-d2d3-4987-82ec-771cc7384ae1)
Notice the left wall now has no rotation at all.

Screenshot 2 - incorrect bake:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4e48840-81ad-4308-a72c-aa1fd40a8245)
I now duplicated the back wall and rotated it 90 and -90 degrees for the left and right walls.



### Steps to reproduce

- Setup a scene with some modular pieces
- Add a light to the scene
- Add a VoxelGI node to the scene
- hit bake
- Now rotate 1 of you walls 180 degrees around the y-axis
- bake again and notice the different result

summary: Editor crashes when callback for `RD::texture_get_data_async` is an empty method

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducable in 4.4-beta2, 4.4-beta1, 4.4-dev7

(All versions with [#100110](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/100110))

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta2 - Windows 11 (build 22631) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-12400F (12 threads)

### Issue description

When calling `RD::texture_get_data_async` from a tool script on every process and passing a callback to an empty method the Editor crashes after about 5 seconds.

```
ERROR: FATAL: Index p_index = 8278 is out of bounds (count = 82).
   at: operator[] (./core/templates/local_vector.h:178)

================================================================
CrashHandlerException: Program crashed with signal 4
Engine version: Godot Engine v4.4.beta2.official (a013481b0911e59cc3f3dea7ebb732450c3e1460)
Dumping the backtrace. Please include this when reporting the bug to the project developer.

(no backtrace.. same with --verbose)
```

Running the game does not trigger the crash. It only seems to be happening in the editor.

Having something happen in the callback also doesn't seem to trigger the crash or perhaps delays it to great extend. I doubt that the empty callback is the root cause. It probably just triggers whatever is wrong with using it in the editor faster. Though, I'm speculating here.

If this wasn't intended to work in the editor, there should be a guard.

### Steps to reproduce

- Uncommennt `@tool` at the top of node.gd
- save
- reopen the scene
- switch to 2D
- wait for about 5s

Also try uncommenting the print statement in the callback. That seems to stop the crash.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

summary: Exported array in inspector has slider for array size

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.beta.custom_build [eee39f004]

### System information

Godot v4.4.beta (eee39f004) - macOS Sequoia (15.3.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon Pro 5500 XT OpenGL Engine - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10700K CPU @ 3.80GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

The array size field in the editor inspector includes a slider, which is effectively useless because it has such a large range:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/99cebd43-0dd0-4e40-9eba-456725123e22

[In `editor/editor_properties_array_dict.cpp` at line 449, we can see that its maximum value is indeed `INT32_MAX`](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/eee39f004b42a4948af90cdebedf65c34a4a4970/editor/editor_properties_array_dict.cpp#L449):
```cpp
size_slider->set_max(INT32_MAX);
```

This number is so large that even with the slider just *barely* moved off to the right, the array is being given over 5,000,000 elements. There is also noticeable lag in the editor as this is done.

### Steps to reproduce

Create an exported array in a GDScript class:
```gdscript
@export var my_array: Array[int] = []
```

Then, attach it to a node in the editor. Select the node and expand the array, and observe the slider on the "Size:" field. Try to drag it, and notice that the array size becomes *very* large.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Popups are not blocking the mouse click signal from moving further when closing the popup by clicking outside the popup.


### System information

Using 4.4 beta 2, Windows 11

### Issue description

In 3.x, the Popup windows work as expected, which is: When a popup opens, it will stay open until the user clicks outside of the popup. Then, it closes the popup BUT does not propagate the mouse click further down. This is very important because a user who wants to close the popup doesn't have to worry about where they click to close it.

However, in 4.x, mouse clicks outside the popup are closing the popup BUT are passing through the click and activating items underneath. This limits where the user can click to close the popup. 

### Steps to reproduce

Run MRP and click on the button to open the popup.
Click outside the popup to close it.
Open the popup again and this time click over the toggle button.
The toggle button should not be activated at all.
However, the popup closes AND the toggle button is pressed

### Issue description

Tested on all 3 versions of Godot I currently have downloaded.

I have an invisible wall (CollisionShape3D, under a StaticBody3D) that I want to use to block the player when a specific set of code is triggered via Area3D. Prior to this occurring, the CollisionShape3D's "disabled" property is set to true in _ready().

When the player triggers the code via Area3D, the CollisionShape3D's disabled property is set to false. While the disabled property is indeed false, the player can still walk through the collision shape.

Modifying the disabled property via remote inspector or via some toggle code using the T key works fine, the CollisionShape3D will block the player's path as intended.

If this is intended Engine behavior, I've done something wrong, or there is already an issue open to solve this, please let me know. I looked, but did not see an open or closed issue related to this bug. I apologize if I missed it.

### Steps to reproduce

Please use the minimal reproduction project. I have added instructions inside of it via label3d.

However, if you would rather read a step-by-step here, please do the following inside the project:
1. Run the scene with F6
2. Turn to your immediate right. Walk into the flat cube (Area3D) to enable the CollisionShape3D and show its MeshInstance3D.
3. Verify that the CollisionShape3D's disabled property is set to false in the remote inspector. Parent node of the CollisionShape3D is Collider_StaticBody3D.
4. Try walking through the CollisionShape3D. You should go through it, even though it's supposed to block your path.
5. Use the "T" key to disable the CollisionShape3D. Press "T" again to bring it back. Try walking through the CollisionShape3D. It will now block your path.
6. Use the "T" key to disable the CollisionShape3D. Walk through the Area3D from step 2 again to enable the CollisionShape3D. Try waling through the CollisionShape3D. Once again, it will not block your path.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[mrp_collisionshape3d_disabled_toggle_bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18646545/mrp_collisionshape3d_disabled_toggle_bug.zip)

Allows viewing the default values ​​of the currently used theme item in the Inspector.

For theme item properties of resource type, editing the default resource does not make sense. Use **Make Unique** to create a copy before editing.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

https://docs.godotengine.org/en/latest/contributing/development/code_style_guidelines.html

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d01c51d0-bb3b-44b5-aba1-a8980db829b4

The value field displays the value of the currently **used** theme item, regardless of whether it is checked or not.

- When _unchecked_, it simply displays the value **used** by the current theme item. No override is set at this time. This usually means that the property value is **Not Set** or **inhert** (in the control).
- When _checked_, the setting override is displayed, which is also the currently **used** theme item.

summary: Sometimes when exporting in "--headless" mode, shaders with "global uniform" doesn't work

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproduced: v4.3.stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable.mono - Windows 10.0.19045 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6094) - AMD Ryzen 5 1600 Six-Core Processor (12 Threads)

### Issue description

Similar to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/102362, which seems only visual, but in some cases might change into completely breaking a shader.
The issue happens when you have shader with global uniforms (correctly added to the Project Settings) and export it.

Behavior of exporting the project normally or via cmd and that of exporting it **via cmd with --headless** mode is different. Doing it the first method works correctly, doing it the second method breaks the shaders with global uniforms. 

But importantly, the working of the exported shader will be based on **how the project was exported for the first time** 

So:
1. Export normally -> shader works in the build
2. Export with --headless -> shader still work in the build

BUT:
1. Export with --headless -> shader does NOT work in the build
2. **Export normally -> shader STILL does NOT work in the build**

I traced this behavior to the ".godot/exported" folder - if you remove it, you "restart" the process, as above.

I noticed it first when setting up my CI pipeline (with Github Actions). As there is no .godot folder on the machine, and it uses --headless, it's gonna break the shaders every time. Only fix I found was including .godot/exported in the source control.


### Steps to reproduce

1. In empty project, create shader that uses "global uniform". Then create material using that shader and apply it on some mesh (not sure if required)
2. Add the required global uniforms in Project Settings
3. Remove the  ".godot/exported" folder (if exists)
4. Export the project using command line using "--headless" mode. (In case of MRP I use godot --headless --export-release "Windows Desktop" "build/Shader Test.exe")
5. Notice the shader does not use the uniform correctly in the build

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[shader-test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18642930/shader-test.zip)

summary: Label3D outline has transparency sorting issues even when it's solid color

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.3

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.19045 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600H with Radeon Graphics (12 Threads)

### Issue description

The outline of Label3Ds has transparency sorting issues, even when the outline is set to a solid color.

In all examples below, Label3D has default settings (solid colors, Alpha cut disabled).

Exhibit A: a mesh and a Sprite3D with transparency. The labels that are drawn in front of the objects have their outline's color affected by the objects behind them, which shouldn't happen with a solid color. You may also notice that the editor's grid bleeds through the outline.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/577080f1-a778-4e66-a1e5-0e646b2eefb7)

Exhibit B: a mesh and a Sprite3D with a simple shader. The labels in front of the objects consistently lose their outline. The labels behind the objects may or may not render their "covered" portion depending on the camera angle (which is an issue as well), but they still lose the outline.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ec438d1f-8def-4ad8-b62b-1c5117e1a319)

Exhibit C: Same shader, but this time I changed the settings on the Labels. The label behind the sprite has rendering priority -1 for the text and -2 for the outline. The one in front has 3 and 2, respectively. I don't believe this to be a viable long-term solution because objects tend to move.
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/90a5baa0-2c15-436c-888e-ddb2ee15cbfd)

I believe this to be something that needs to be fixed on engine level, because:
1. otherwise the default settings provide the best visual result compared to alpha hash and discard,
2. the user can't control the outline's rendering behavior separately, despite the fact that it behaves differently than the text.

Might be related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/77153

### Steps to reproduce

Open the MRP and just fly around the scene in the editor. Or:

1. Create a Label3D with the default settings
2. Create a visual object with transparency. A half-transparent mesh/sprite and a mesh/sprite with a shader that writes to alpha will have different results (see the showcase above).
3. Place the label in front of the object.
